news.title: Five private-run property registration hubs to open in August, one in Pune
news.para: Maharashtra's registration department is launching five private model sub-registrar offices in August, including one in Pune, as part of a plan for 60 such centers via public-private partnership. These modern facilities, modeled on Passport Seva Kendras, will offer improved infrastructure and services for an additional fee. While private operators manage facilities, government officials retain all statutory powers, ensuring continued regulatory control.

news.title: Bhopal: EOW books Hilltop Society office-bearers in multi-crore plot fraud case
news.para: Bhopal's Economic Offences Wing has filed a fraud case against office-bearers of the Hilltop Housing Cooperative Society. For over four decades, the society allegedly collected crores from hundreds of members for promised plots but failed to deliver possession. Investigations revealed evidence of cheating, forgery, and criminal conspiracy, with members facing years of delays and alleged manipulation of records, even after land acquisition by the MP Housing Board.

news.title: Institutional ownership narrows office supply as outright demand holds up
news.para: India’s office real estate market is shifting towards a leasing-led model, with developers, REITs and institutional investors increasingly retaining assets instead of selling them, reducing the availability of premium office spaces for outright purchase.

news.title: Chennai development body to exit construction, stick to urban planning
news.para: Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority (CMDA) is exiting construction after a ₹3,000 crore expenditure led to a ₹1,500 crore deficit.  The authority, which has exhausted its corpus, will refocus on its core mandate of urban planning, approvals, and creating new neighborhoods.  This strategic shift aims to revive CMDA's financial health and return to its foundational role in city development.

news.title: As flights take off at Noida airport, focus now shifts to Aerocity
news.para: Noida International Airport's launch is igniting a massive transformation, turning surrounding land into a vibrant aerotropolis.  Plans include hotels, malls, and business districts, creating a self-contained airport city.  This development, alongside significant industrial growth, is poised to generate thousands of jobs and establish the region as a major investment hub for the National Capital Region.

news.title: Over 41 lakh houses built for poor: Bihar IPRD minister
news.para: Bihar has achieved a significant milestone, constructing over 41.56 lakh permanent homes for the underprivileged since the 2016-17 fiscal year. This initiative, a revamped version of the Indira Awas Yojana now known as Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Gramin), provides crucial financial aid of Rs 1.20 lakh per beneficiary. The program aims to transform the lives of countless homeless families by enabling them to own a safe and dignified dwelling.

news.title: Over 300 beneficiaries in Jamshedpur receive keys of new homes developed under PMAY
news.para: Over 300 families in Jamshedpur's Birsanagar received keys to their new homes under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana. Jharkhand's Urban Development Minister inaugurated the event online, highlighting the project's role in providing safe and dignified living for the economically weaker sections. This initiative marks a significant step in urban development, aiming to improve living standards and fulfill the government's 'home for all' commitment.

news.title: Odisha government issues SOP for apartment registration
news.para: Apartment registrations in Odisha are set for a major overhaul with a new Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) introduced by the revenue and housing departments. This move aims to eliminate confusion arising from existing laws, ensuring a clearer and standardized process for buyers. The SOP categorizes registrations, outlines required documents, and clarifies verification steps for different types of apartment sales, bringing much-needed transparency to property transactions.

news.title: India needs affordable housing push, rental reforms to reach $5.8 trillion realty market by 2047: Report
news.para: A KPMG-NAREDCO report has identified affordable housing, rental housing, RERA-IBC alignment and stronger RERA implementation as key policy priorities for India’s real estate sector. The report was released at NAREDCO Real Estate Conclave 2026.

news.title: Indore's Mayor-in-Council rejects property tax re-zoning proposal
news.para: Indore's Mayor-in-Council rejected a property tax hike for over 50 areas, including newly integrated villages. They approved a uniform cinema screen licensing fee and will tender for 8,100 housing units under PMAY 2.0. The council also greenlit regularization of the regulated workforce, expanded medical aid for sanitation and drainage workers, and approved infrastructure projects like sewer network shifts and water tank installations.

news.title: UP-RERA makes quarterly transaction reporting mandatory for real estate agents
news.para: Registered real estate agents in Uttar Pradesh will have to maintain books of accounts and customer transaction records in prescribed formats. Delay in filing quarterly transaction reports will attract a fee of ₹10,000 for the corresponding quarter.

news.title: Punjab government begins auction of VAT, GST defaulters' properties to recover pending dues
news.para: Punjab has intensified its crackdown on tax defaulters, initiating public auctions of properties belonging to chronic VAT and GST evaders. The state has already recovered significant sums through major property sales in Mohali and Jalandhar. This aggressive enforcement aims to reclaim pending revenue, with the One Time Settlement Scheme extended until July 31, 2026, offering a final chance for compliance before further stringent actions are taken against remaining evaders.

news.title: AI, sustainability to shape real estate growth: Experts
news.para: India's real estate sector is poised for significant expansion, driven by artificial intelligence, sustainable building, and regulatory adherence, according to developers at the NAREDCO Real Estate Conclave 2026. Experts emphasized the crucial role of RERA in fostering trust and highlighted emerging opportunities in commercial spaces and data centers.  Addressing a skilled workforce shortage and promoting faster construction methods were also key discussion points for future growth.

news.title: Mahindra Lifespaces acquires 15-acre land in Mumbai; YKK India to set up plant at Chennai industrial park
news.para: Mahindra Lifespace Developers has acquired a 15-acre land parcel in Kandivali East, Mumbai, with an estimated gross development value of ₹5,600 crore. Separately, YKK India will set up a manufacturing facility at Origins by Mahindra, Chennai, with an investment of $150 million.

news.title: Consumer panel orders builder to refund ₹12.5 lakh with interest to Lonavala plot buyer
news.para: Mumbai residents will receive a Rs 12.5 lakh refund with 12% annual interest from Emnoy Properties India LLP for a Lonavala bungalow plot. The Maharashtra State Consumer Commission also awarded Rs 2 lakh for mental agony and Rs 50,000 for costs. Buyers alleged project delays and title disputes after paying Rs 12.5 lakh in 2012, with development failing to progress and no alternative plots materializing.

news.title: Office REIT penetration may rise to 30% by 2030: Report
news.para: Listed REITs and InvITs with real estate assets have an operational portfolio of over 195 million sq ft as of March 2026, according to Colliers India. Office assets under REITs currently stand at about 164 million sq ft, translating into a penetration level of around 19%.

news.title: Hinjewadi-Shivajinagar metro line on track for August commissioning: PITCMRL
news.para: Pune's much-awaited Metro Line 3 is set to open in August 2026. The first phase, covering 12 stations from Maan to RK Laxman Museum, will be ready by June-end. This new line will significantly reduce travel time between Shivajinagar and Hinjewadi.  It promises seamless connectivity and a sustainable transport alternative for thousands of commuters.

news.title: Chennai metro phase-II line yet to open despite getting approval
news.para: Chennai Metro Rail's first Phase-2 line, a 14.6km stretch from Poonamallee Bypass to Vadapalani, has received all necessary approvals but remains uncommissioned four months later. Delays, attributed to elections and government formation, have pushed back the planned launch, leaving commuters in western suburbs waiting for improved connectivity.

news.title: Premium FAR scheme not Akrama-Sakrama by another name: Karnataka HC
news.para: Bengaluru's High Court has upheld the state's Premium Floor Area Ratio scheme. The court ruled it is different from the Akrama-Sakrama scheme. Premium FAR allows increased construction by altering development rules. It does not regularise illegal structures. The scheme benefits new and existing buildings. Transferable Development Rights will remain in demand.

news.title: No new Bengaluru metro constructions in 4.5 years as approvals lag
news.para: Bengaluru's Metro expansion faces a significant halt, with no new corridors entering construction for over four years. Key Phase III and IIIA projects are stalled in the approval pipeline, exacerbated by design changes like double-decker corridors, leading to substantial cost escalations and delayed public transport development.
